target.name: Rain Industries
target.founder: Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, Maxwell Brodie

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rain Industries is a seed-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, and Maxwell Brodie. The company has raised $14.7M to date. The drones and autonomous sector comprises seven private companies and no public comparables; key peers include Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The stage-progression method uses Rain's $15M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage transitions in this sector, yielding a median valuation of $37M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient comparable data points at the same or adjacent stage within the drones and autonomous sector; while four same-sector peers exist, none met the filters for stage, proximity, or valuation tier needed to support a reliable median.

The triangulated valuation range is $31M to $42M, centered on $37M, supported by the single applicable methodology. The primary uncertainty stems from limited comparable transaction data in the seed stage of this emerging sector. A fuller range could be narrowed with additional seed-stage peer transactions or clarity on Rain's near-term financing trajectory and revenue traction relative to the broader peer set.
